tags.notes: Garden Eel Cove (Makako Bay) — primary Kona manta site. Sandy bottom at ~9m. Reef manta (Mobula alfredi). 450+ catalogued resident rays; 80-90% sighting success rate year-round.

Marine Life Conservation District established 1969, 315 acres. Ka'awaloa Cove near Cook Monument. Depth 5-36m. Spinner dolphins documented. 100+ fish species. Boat access only — motorised vessel moratorium enforced.

Shore dive at Crescent Beach (Alua/Dog Beach/Nai'a Bay). Depth 0-27m (90ft). Tiger sharks (Galeocerdo cuvier) are documented residents; moray eels, octopus, colorful reef fish. Active boat channel — intermediate navigation required. Known by multiple names: Manta Ray Bay, Nai'a Bay, Crescent Beach, Alua Beach.

Also called Manta Ray Heaven / Makako Bay. Hawaiian garden eels (Gorgasia hawaiiensis) in sand at ~24m (80ft). Campfire/night-dive viewing area at ~10m (35ft). Kona Honu Divers exclusively uses this site since 2018. Protected from south swell by Keahole Point. Site established for manta viewing in August 1999.
Lava tube/cavern system on North Kona Coast. Surge-dependent dive. Standard morning two-tank site. Spiny lobsters, porcupine pufferfish, octopus documented by multiple operator/forum sources.

Series of lava fingers with arches, tubes, caves. Reef top ~3m, drops to ~18m, then deeper. Hawaiian green sea turtle (Chelonia mydas) cleaning station confirmed. White-tip reef sharks in pukas (caves). Spotted eagle rays documented. Multiple entry points along Puako Beach Drive. Difficulty upgraded to Intermediate: wind-driven surface chop, navigation over long shallow reef, active boat traffic concern.

Umbrella entry covering Black Point, Frog Rock, Lava Dome (primary sites). Frog Rock: 3-20m, lava tubes with interconnecting caverns, porcupine puffers, octopus, strong currents possible. Lava Dome: 9-30m, centuries-old coral head, 'Pink Floyd' ledge with schooling fish. Marine life confirmed: white-mouth moray eels, leaf scorpionfish, frogfish, arc-eye hawkfish, snowball nudibranch, marbled shrimp, blue dragon nudibranch, red soldierfish. site_type updated to cave (lava tube/cavern primary character).

South Kona near Miloli'i. Lava tube ~40ft (13m) long decorated with Tubastrea coral. Resident frogfish (3 reported). Green turtles documented. Far from shore — primarily liveaboard or long-range boat access. Water temp slightly cooler than Kona average (~24°C/75°F).

North Kona coast. Seamount rises to within ~7m (25ft) of surface. Max depth ~18m. Natural cleaning station for Hawaiian green sea turtles (Chelonia mydas). Cleaner fish: surgeonfish/tang (family Acanthuridae). Visibility 45-60m. Light currents typical. Suitable all cert levels.

Miloli'i Bay area. Collapsed lava tube grottoes with skylights. Max depth ~12m (40ft). Hawaiian green turtles (Chelonia mydas) abundant and accustomed to divers. Fish life described by multiple sources as 'average' — name refers to topography not fish density.
